PLSC - Plant Sciences

PLSC400 Plant Physiology (4 Credits) An in-depth examination of the unique molecular and physiological principles necessary to understand how plants grow and respond to the environment at the cellular and organismal levels. Plants evolved unique metabolism and survival strategies, so students should be prepared to enter a world that may be new to them. Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in BSCI170 and BSCI171; or minimum grade of C- in PLSC201; and minimum grade of C- in CHEM231 and CHEM232; or minimum grade of C- in CHEM237. Cross-listed with: BSCI442. Credit Only Granted for: BSCI442 or PLSC400.

PLSC410 Commercial Turf Maintenance and Production (3 Credits) Agronomic programs and practices used in hydroseeding, commercial lawn care, sod production and seed production. Current environmental, regulatory and business management issues confronting the turfgrass industry. Prerequisite: PLSC305; or permission of AGNR-Plant Science & Landscape Architecture department.
